daily_brief: The US House of Representatives passed a war powers resolution seeking to halt military action in Iran, with four Republicans joining Democrats in a 215-208 vote that marks growing bipartisan opposition to President Trump's conduct of the conflict [BBC, Globe and Mail, The Hindu]. Meanwhile, Israel and Lebanon announced a conditional ceasefire agreement following US-mediated talks in Washington, though Israeli Defense Minister Katz immediately stated military operations would continue in southern Lebanon, and strikes resumed Thursday morning [RFI, BBC, France 24, Le Monde, Financial Times]. In eastern DRC, rebel attacks by the Islamic State-affiliated ADF killed over 30 people near Beni, severely complicating the Ebola outbreak response that has now claimed 60 lives with 344 confirmed cases [The Guardian]. The WHO revealed the outbreak may have begun as early as January, giving it a 'big head start.' Germany suffered a setback at the UN, losing its Security Council bid to Portugal and Austria, with Foreign Minister Wadephul citing Russian opposition and Germany's support for Ukraine and Israel as factors [BBC, The Hindu]. Russia increased pressure on Armenia ahead of Sunday's parliamentary elections, threatening gas price hikes and trade restrictions as Yerevan pursues closer EU ties [France 24].

top_stories.headline: US House Passes War Powers Resolution to Halt Iran Conflict in Rebuke to Trump
bullets: The House voted 215-208 to pass a resolution requiring Trump to withdraw forces from Iran or seek congressional approval, with four Republicans joining all Democrats
This marks the fourth House attempt to curb Trump's war powers and the first successful vote; the Senate advanced a similar resolution in May but has not held a full floor vote
Iran-US ceasefire talks remain stalled, with Tehran saying 'no tangible progress' was made while an Iranian drone struck Kuwait airport Wednesday, killing one person
The resolution is largely symbolic as Trump can veto it, but it signals growing bipartisan opposition amid high gas prices and public disapproval of the war

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized the political significance and divisions within the Republican Party

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Focused on implications for US negotiating position with Iran and historical parallels to Vietnam

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Highlighted the symbolic nature and linked to ongoing violence including Kuwait airport strike

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Brief coverage emphasizing Trump's ability to veto

top_stories.headline: Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire Announced but Israeli Military Operations Continue
bullets: Israel and Lebanon agreed to implement a conditional ceasefire after fourth round of US-mediated talks in Washington, contingent on Hezbollah halting attacks
The agreement includes creating 'pilot zones' where Lebanese armed forces would take exclusive control, excluding all non-state actors
Israeli Defense Minister Katz announced Thursday that military operations would continue and forces would remain in southern Lebanon's 'security zone'
Israeli strikes hit southern Lebanon Thursday morning despite the agreement; Hezbollah has not commented on the deal and continues to reject direct Israel-Lebanon talks

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Led with strikes continuing despite truce announcement

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Provided detailed background on Hezbollah and comprehensive coverage of agreement terms

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Katz's statement on continued operations

top_stories.headline: Rebel Attacks Kill Over 30 in Eastern DRC, Disrupting Ebola Response
bullets: Allied Democratic Forces (ADF), an Islamic State-affiliated militia, killed over 30 people in attacks on villages around Beni in North Kivu province over several days
The attacks included beheadings, shootings, burning of homes and motorcycles, and kidnappings of civilians; hundreds fled into the bush
The Ebola outbreak has reached 344 confirmed cases and 60 deaths in DRC, plus 15 cases and one death in Uganda
WHO chief Tedros said the outbreak may have begun in January, and contact tracing remains critically low at 45% due to insecurity and displacement

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Comprehensive coverage linking security crisis to public health emergency; also covered WHO statements and US quarantine controversy in Kenya

top_stories.headline: Germany Loses UN Security Council Bid to Portugal and Austria
bullets: Germany secured only 104 votes in the UN General Assembly election for non-permanent Security Council seats, losing to Portugal (134 votes) and Austria (131 votes)
Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul blamed Russia for lobbying against Germany, citing Berlin's firm support for Ukraine
Wadephul also suggested Germany's special responsibility toward Israel in the Middle East conflict may have cost votes among UN member states
Austria, Kyrgyzstan, Portugal, Trinidad and Tobago, and Zimbabwe were elected for 2027-28 terms

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Focused on Wadephul's attribution of blame to Russia and Germany's support positions

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Emphasized the competitive nature of the election and included India's congratulatory statement

top_stories.headline: Russia Escalates Pressure on Armenia Ahead of Crucial Parliamentary Election
bullets: Russia threatened to raise gas prices, restrict Armenian product imports, and suspend Armenia from the Eurasian Economic Union ahead of June 7 elections
Armenia currently pays $177.50 per 1,000 cubic meters of Russian gas versus over $600 in Europe; 82% of Armenian gas comes from Russia
Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan's government has publicly criticized Moscow and pursued closer ties with the EU and Washington
The election pits Pashinyan's Civil Contract party against multiple opposition voices, many of them pro-Russian

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Detailed coverage of specific Russian trade threats and their timing relative to the election

additional_stories.headline: French-Iranian author Marjane Satrapi, creator of 'Persepolis,' dies at 56
France's parliament unanimously acknowledges state responsibility for chlordecone pesticide contamination in Caribbean territories
Ghana passes sweeping law criminalizing LGBTQ+ activity with prison sentences of 3-10 years
Corsica autonomy plan clears first parliamentary hurdle in French National Assembly
China bans four New Zealand lawmakers over visit to Taiwan, calling it interference in internal affairs
Five Eyes intelligence alliance warns China's military is recruiting through job sites and LinkedIn to extract sensitive information
US proposes 12.5% tariff on 54 countries including New Zealand and Australia over alleged failure to prevent forced labor imports
Ukraine strikes St. Petersburg oil terminal and naval base hours before opening of major Russian economic forum
At least 11 killed in fire at elderly care home in Sri Lanka's Kalutara district
Bangladesh Foreign Minister Khalilur Rahman elected 81st UN General Assembly president, defeating Cyprus
